subject: Social Sciences
title: Apartheid Era
tagline: Understand the apartheid system and its impact on South Africans!

stages.name: Stage 1
stages.question: What year did apartheid officially begin?

stages.name: Stage 2
stages.question: What law forced people to live in separate areas by race?
options: The first democratic election for all races
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The Group Areas Act is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The Group Areas Act.

stages.name: Stage 3
stages.question: What document did people have to carry under apartheid?
options: The first democratic election for all races
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A pass (passbook) is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A pass (passbook).

stages.name: Stage 4
stages.question: What was the 1976 student uprising called?
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The Soweto Uprising is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The Soweto Uprising.

stages.name: Stage 5
stages.question: Who led the ANC during much of the anti-apartheid struggle?
options: A designated area for Black South Africans
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Oliver Tambo, then Nelson Mandela is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Oliver Tambo, then Nelson Mandela.

stages.name: Stage 6
stages.question: What year did apartheid officially end?

stages.name: Stage 7
stages.question: What was the armed wing of the ANC called?
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Umkhonto we Sizwe is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Umkhonto we Sizwe.

stages.name: Stage 8
stages.question: What international action did many countries take against apartheid?
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Economic sanctions is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Economic sanctions.

stages.name: Stage 9
stages.question: What commission investigated apartheid-era crimes after 1994?
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The Truth and Reconciliation Commission is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The Truth and Reconciliation Commission.

stages.name: Stage 10
stages.question: Who chaired the Truth and Reconciliation Commission?
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Archbishop Desmond Tutu is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Archbishop Desmond Tutu.

stages.name: Stage 11
stages.question: What day commemorates the Sharpeville Massacre?
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Human Rights Day, 21 March is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Human Rights Day, 21 March.

stages.name: Stage 12
stages.question: What law classified South Africans by race?
options: A designated area for Black South Africans
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The Population Registration Act is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The Population Registration Act.

stages.name: Stage 13
stages.question: What education law limited Black South Africans' schooling?
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The Bantu Education Act is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The Bantu Education Act.

stages.name: Stage 14
stages.question: What was a 'homeland' under apartheid?
options: A designated area for Black South Africans
stages.correctOption: A designated area for Black South Africans
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! A designated area for Black South Africans is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is A designated area for Black South Africans.

stages.name: Stage 15
stages.question: What did the 1994 election mark for South Africa?
options: The first democratic election for all races
stages.correctOption: The first democratic election for all races
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! The first democratic election for all races is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is The first democratic election for all races.
